## 1. Executive verdict and confidence

I can prove, rigorously and unconditionally, that the `b=1` two‚Äësupport problem **decomposes exactly** into (i) two bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix‚Äëfiber problems and (ii) one genuinely new scalar object: the **active‚Äëexternal‚Äëroot count**. This both isolates the smallest algebraic object below `b=1` and shows that `b=1` is *not* equivalent to the full arbitrary‚Äëword local limit, so the "ROUTE_CUT: b=1 ‚ü∫ full" option is **false**. The clean conceptual statement is:

> **Bandwidth = number of root‚Äëconfiguration points allowed off the subgroup `H`.**
> `Fib_U(s)` for `deg U = s+b` is governed by configurations `W` of `s+b` points with `|W ‚à© H| ‚â• s` and prescribed prefix `Œ¶_{œÉ+b}(W)=c`; `b` is the number of points of `W` outside `H`. `b=0` is the pure prefix fiber (all in `H`); `b=1` allows exactly one external point `Œ∏‚ààF_p‚àñH`.

Confidence: **high** on the decomposition theorem (it is elementary polynomial algebra over `F_p`, verifiable by a finite checker). **High** that the residual wall is the active‚Äëexternal‚Äëroot count and that it is fixed‚Äëprime (the norm sieve dies at `b=1` at the *same* `p`-range as `b=0`, so Cycle96 transfers verbatim). **Moderate** that the wall is closable without new equidistribution; I give the exact character sum but do not close it.

---

## 2. Exact statement

**Convention.** `H=‚ü®œâ‚ü©‚âÖŒº_n`, `n=2^m`, `p‚â°1 (mod n)`, `k=œÅn`, `s=k+œÉ`, `œÉ‚â•Cn/log n`, `q_gen=q_line=q_code=q_chal=p` (prime, generated). Residue convention `deg(U mod L_S)<k` (dimension‚Äë`k` code; the prompt's `‚â§k` is the dimension‚Äë`(k+1)` relabeling `œÉ‚Ü¶œÉ‚àí1`, immaterial). WLOG `U` monic of degree `s+1`; let `c=(c_1,‚Ä¶,c_{œÉ+1})` be its top `œÉ+1` subleading coefficients read as elementary symmetric data (`[X^{s+1‚àíj}]U=(‚àí1)^j c_j`, `c_0=1`).

**Theorem B1 (bandwidth‚Äë1 decomposition).**
The list `List(U,1‚àís/n)` of degree‚Äë`<k` codewords `P` agreeing with `U` on `‚â•s` points is in bijection (`P‚Ü¶V:=U‚àíP`) with
```
V = { V monic, deg V = s+1, top œÉ+1 coeffs = those of U, #(roots of V in H) ‚â• s }.
```
Every `V‚ààV` has exactly `s` or `s+1` roots in `H`, giving a disjoint split `V = V_A ‚äî V_B`:

- **`V_A`** (all `s+1` roots in `H`): `V=L_R`, `R‚ààC(H,s+1)`, `Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(R)=c`. This is a **bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix fiber** at `(k,œÉ+1)` on `(s+1)`-subsets.
- **`V_B`** (exactly `s` roots `S‚äÜH`, one external root `Œ∏‚ààF_p‚àñH`): `V=(X‚àíŒ∏)L_S`. The external root is *forced* by the leading subleading coefficient,
```
            Œ∏ = c_1 ‚àí e_1(S),
```
and for each fixed `Œ∏` the slice `{S}` is the **bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix fiber** `Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c(Œ∏))` on `C(H,s)` at `(k‚àí1,œÉ+1)`, where
```
            c(Œ∏)_j = Œ£_{i=0}^{j} (‚àíŒ∏)^{j‚àíi} c_i      (j=1,‚Ä¶,œÉ+1),     deg_Œ∏ c(Œ∏)_j = j.
```

Consequently, with `Œò_U := { Œ∏‚ààF_p‚àñH : Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c(Œ∏)) ‚âÝ ‚àÖ }` the **active‚Äëexternal‚Äëroot set**, and writing `Œì(Œ∏)=(c(Œ∏)_1,‚Ä¶,c(Œ∏)_{œÉ+1})` for the explicit **degree‚Äë`(œÉ+1)` external‚Äëroot curve** in `F_p^{œÉ+1}`,
```
   |List(U,1‚àís/n)|  ‚â§  |Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c)|_{(k,œÉ+1)}            (Type A, b=0)
                    +  Œ£_{Œ∏‚ààŒò_U} |Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c(Œ∏))|_{(k‚àí1,œÉ+1)}   (Type B, each b=0)
```
and `|Fib_U(s)| ‚â§ (s+1)¬∑|List(U,1‚àís/n)|`, `#{S' : S‚ñ≥S' aperiodic} ‚â§ (s+1)¬∑|List^{aper}(U)|`.

**Corollary (the wall is one scalar).** Granting the bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix local limit (every aperiodic prefix fiber `‚â§ n^{B_0}` after charging quotient cores ‚Äî this is `conj:prefix-local`/`thm:upstairs`, proven for `p>exp(O((log n)^2))` by `thm:no-collision`), then
```
   |List^{aper}(U)|  ‚â§  n^{B_0} ¬∑ ( 1 + |Œò_U^{aper}| ).
```
Therefore **S1 for `b=1` ‚ü∫ ( bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix local limit ) ‚àß ( |Œò_U^{aper}| ‚â§ n^{O(1)} )**. The first conjunct is the existing `b=0` wall; the second is the new object.

**`EXACT_NEW_WALL` (S1‚ÄëB1‚ÄëWALL).** For `deg U=s+1`, after charging quotient‚Äëperiodic fibers,
```
   |Œò_U^{aper}|  =  | Œì ‚à© Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(C(H,s)) |   ‚â§   n^{O(1)},
```
i.e. the explicit degree‚Äë`(œÉ+1)` curve `Œì‚äÇF_p^{œÉ+1}` meets the prefix image of `s`-subsets of `H` in polynomially many points. Equivalently (power sums, `p>œÉ+1`): writing `u=p_1(S)` free, `S` must satisfy the `œÉ` constraints `p_j(S)=P_j‚àí(P_1‚àíu)^j` for `2‚â§j‚â§œÉ+1` ‚Äî a prefix target sliding along a **degree‚Äë`(œÉ+1)` algebraic curve** parametrized by the single freedom `u`.

## 3. Proof of Theorem B1, and the obstruction at the wall

**Proof of B1.** `P‚Ü¶V=U‚àíP` is an affine bijection from `{deg P<k}` onto `{monic deg s+1 with top œÉ+1 coeffs = U's}`, because `deg U=s+1>k>deg P` forces `deg V=s+1` and matching of all coefficients in degrees `k,‚Ä¶,s+1` (that is `œÉ+1` of them, namely `e_1,‚Ä¶,e_{œÉ+1}`). Agreement of `P` with `U` on `S` ‚ü∫ `V` vanishes on `S` ‚ü∫ `S‚äÜ` roots of `V`. Since `deg V=s+1`, `V` has `‚â§s+1` roots, so a listed `V` has `s` or `s+1` roots in `H`; the agreement set has size `‚â§ s+1`, giving `binom(|Agr|,s)‚â§s+1` subsets per codeword, hence `|Fib_U(s)|‚â§(s+1)|List|`.

*Type A.* `s+1` roots in `H` ‚üπ `V=L_R`, `R‚ààC(H,s+1)`; the prescribed `e_1,‚Ä¶,e_{œÉ+1}` of `V` are `Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(R)=c`. Pure `b=0`.

*Type B.* `V=(X‚àíŒ∏)L_S`, `S‚äÜH`, `Œ∏‚àâH`. Then `e_j(V)=e_j(S‚à™{Œ∏})=e_j(S)+Œ∏e_{j‚àí1}(S)`. The constraint `e_1(V)=c_1` gives `Œ∏=c_1‚àíe_1(S)`; recursively `e_j(S)=c_j‚àíŒ∏e_{j‚àí1}(S)`, so fixing `Œ∏` prescribes the entire prefix `(e_1(S),‚Ä¶,e_{œÉ+1}(S))=c(Œ∏)`, a `b=0` fiber on `C(H,s)`. Solving the recursion gives the closed form `c(Œ∏)_j=Œ£_{i=0}^j(‚àíŒ∏)^{j‚àíi}c_i`. ‚àé

**Why the wall is genuinely new (not reducible to `b=0`).** The fixed‚Äë`Œ∏` slices are each `b=0`, but `Œ∏` ranges over the **size‚Äë`p` continuum** `F_p‚àñH`. Picking one `S_Œ∏` per active `Œ∏` yields distinct residue codewords, hence pairwise `|S_Œ∏‚à©S_{Œ∏'}|‚â§k‚àí1`: a constant‚Äëweight code of length `n`, weight `s`, distance `‚â•2(œÉ+1)`. Such codes are exponentially large by Gilbert‚ÄìVarshamov, so the pairwise/BCH shadow (`sec:pairwise`, `lem:pairwise`) **cannot** bound `|Œò_U|`. The bound must come from the simultaneous algebraic constraint that *all* configurations share the prefix `c` along the single curve `Œì` ‚Äî a fixed‚Äëprime incidence between a degree‚Äë`(œÉ+1)` curve and the combinatorial set `Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(C(H,s))`, which is exactly the technology `prob:perfiber` names (FFT‚Äëgrid character sums on `H`).

**Why the norm sieve does not help (consistency with Cycle96).** A Type‚ÄëB configuration `S‚à™{Œ∏}` lifted to `Z[Œ∂]` carries the arbitrary‚Äëword coset height `‚â•p^{1‚àíœÅ‚àío(1)}` (`L-CYCLE96-WORD-HEIGHT-FLOOR`) in the `S`-part, *plus* an uncontrolled external coordinate `Œ∏‚ààF_p` with no cyclotomic structure at all. `lem:galois-amp` still needs height `<p^{(œÉ+1)/n}/n`. So the norm contradiction fails at `b=1` at exactly the same `p`-range as `b=0`: **bandwidth‚Äë1 raises neither the prime threshold nor the height floor.** The wall is purely fixed‚Äëprime, confirming `L-CYCLE96-NORM-SIEVE-IS-DEAD` transfers.

**Why this is not equivalent to the full word.** For `deg U=s+b` the same argument gives configurations `W` with `|W|=s+b`, `|W‚à©H|‚â•s`, `Œ¶_{œÉ+b}(W)=c`, with **up to `b` external points**. The bandwidth filtration `F_b` adds external points one at a time; the full word (`deg U=n‚àí1`) has `b=m‚àí1=n‚àís‚àí1` external points and an `(m‚àí1)`-dimensional external family, versus the `1`-dimensional curve `Œì` here. So `b=1` is strictly the "one external point" layer: the ROUTE_CUT claiming `b=1‚â°full` is **false**.

---

## 4. Verification requirements

All finite, deterministic, in the existing toy range (extend `cycle96_word_height_floor_toy.py`):

1. **Decomposition checker.** For small `p‚â°1 mod n`, `n‚àà{8,16}`, random monic `U` of degree `s+1`: enumerate `List(U,1‚àís/n)` directly by trying all `P` of degree `<k`; independently enumerate `V_A` (prefix fibers on `(s+1)`-subsets) and `V_B` (for each `Œ∏‚ààF_p‚àñH`, the prefix fiber `Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c(Œ∏))` on `s`-subsets). Assert the two counts and the bijection `P‚Ü¶V` agree, and `Œ∏=c_1‚àíe_1(S)` holds for every Type‚ÄëB solution. This certifies Theorem B1.
2. **Active‚Äëroot growth.** For a sweep of `(n,k,œÉ)` along the reserve `œÉ‚âàCn/log n` and increasing `p`, log `|Œò_U|` and `|Œò_U^{aper}|` (after removing `Œ∏` whose fiber is a `Œº_M`-coset class with `M>œÉ`). Test whether `|Œò_U^{aper}|` stays `‚â§ poly(n)` (evidence for PROOF) or grows superpolynomially (COUNTERPACKET).
3. **Curve‚Äëimage incidence.** Directly compute `|Œì ‚à© Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(C(H,s))|` and compare with the random prediction `‚âà binom(n,s)/p^œÉ`‚Äëscale to detect structured `U` that inflate the incidence on a coset.

---

## 5. Next exact lemma / construction

The route to a full solve is: **(a)** bandwidth‚Äë0 prefix local limit (existing); **(b)** for each `b`, the active external‚Äëconfiguration bound; **(c)** induction on `b` to `b=m‚àí1`. The next exact lemma is the `b=1` instance:

> **Lemma S1‚ÄëB1 (to prove or kill).** There is `B=B(œÅ)` such that for `œÉ‚â•Cn/log n`, all `p‚â°1 mod n` with `n^{c_0}‚â§p‚â§2^{o(n)}`, and every monic `U` of degree `s+1`,
> ```
>   #{ Œ∏‚ààF_p‚àñH : Œ¶_{œÉ+1}^{-1}(c(Œ∏)) ‚âÝ ‚àÖ, fiber not a single Œº_M-coset class (M>œÉ) }  ‚â§ n^B.
> ```

**Exact character‚Äësum form to attack (fixed‚Äëprime, no `Z[Œ∂]` lift).** With `e_Œ∏(j):=Œ£_{x‚ààH}1_S(x)œâ^{jx}` notation replaced by the prefix indicator, `Œ∏` is active iff the system `p_j(S)=P_j‚àí(P_1‚àíu)^j` (`2‚â§j‚â§œÉ+1`, `u=P_1‚àíp_1(S)‚àí... `) has a `0/1` weight‚Äë`s` solution on `H`; counting active `Œ∏` is
```
  |Œò_U| = Œ£_{u‚ààF_p} 1[ ‚àÉ S‚äÜH,|S|=s : p_j(S)=P_j‚àí(P_1‚àíu)^j, j=2,‚Ä¶,œÉ+1 ],
```
and the natural majorant is the even‚Äëmoment / Stepanov count of `s`-subsets whose first `œÉ+1` power sums lie on the rational curve `u‚Ü¶(u,P_2‚àí(P_1‚àíu)^2,‚Ä¶)`. The crisp sublemma that converts this to PROOF: **a Weil/Stepanov bound for the number of `F_p`-points of the curve `Œì` lying in the prefix image, after quotient‚Äëperiodic `Œ∏` are removed** ‚Äî i.e. show `Œì` is not contained in (and meets polynomially) the periodicity strata, the only place the count can blow up.

**Counterpacket shape (to refute).** A monic `U` of degree `s+1` (above reserve, generated prime field) for which `Œì` lies on a `Œº_M`-coset translate forcing superpolynomially many aperiodic active `Œ∏`. By the decomposition this is the *only* way `b=1` can beat `b=0`; the toy sweep in ¬ß4.2 is the decisive test. The two‚Äëmoment fullness theory (`thm:twomoment`) suggests the `j=2` parabola constraint already equidistributes `(p_1,p_2)`, which would *prevent* such a packet ‚Äî but this is exactly the unproven fixed‚Äëprime input.

---

## Self‚Äëaudit

1. **Proved / not proved.** Proved: the exact bandwidth‚Äë1 decomposition `List(U)‚âÖV_A‚äîV_B` with `V_A` a `b=0` fiber at `(k,œÉ+1)`, `V_B` a `Œ∏`-union of `b=0` fibers at `(k‚àí1,œÉ+1)`, `Œ∏=c_1‚àíe_1(S)`, external curve degree `œÉ+1`; and the reduction "S1 for `b=1` ‚ü∫ (`b=0` local limit) ‚àß (`|Œò_U^{aper}|‚â§n^{O(1)}`)". Not proved: the active‚Äëroot bound `|Œò_U^{aper}|‚â§n^{O(1)}` itself, and hence not the `b=1` collision bound; no counterpacket produced.
2. **Prize relevance.** Upper‚Äëside, direction of `Œµ_mca` over `thm:normalform`, `q_chal=q_line=q=p`, target `|L_w|‚â§2^{-128}q`. It kills a candidate shortcut (`b=1‚â°full`) and isolates the exact missing scalar; it is a route‚Äëstructuring lemma + restated wall, **not** a finite prize certificate and **not** a counterpacket.
3. **First line that could fail.** `L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST` and Theorem B1 are unconditional. The first failure point is **the active‚Äëexternal‚Äëroot bound** `|Œì‚à©Œ¶_{œÉ+1}(C(H,s))|‚â§n^{O(1)}` ‚Äî the genuinely new fixed‚Äëprime incidence ‚Äî and, upstream of it, the `b=0` prefix local limit at the polynomial‚Äëfield reserve (open beyond `p>exp((log n)^2)`).
4. **Field/`2^{-128}` usage.** Correct. Slopes `z=P(Œ±)/B‚ààF_p`; list normalized by `q=q_line=q_code=q_chal=p`; covering‚Äëradius/height and the curve `Œì` live in the generated field `q_gen=q_D=p`; `2^{-128}` enters as `|List|‚â§2^{-128}p`, which B1 reduces to `b=0`-fibers `+` `|Œò_U^{aper}|`. No ledger merge; binomial reserve not conflated with corrected `œÑ*`/quotient reserve.
5. **Numerator reductions.** Quotient/periodic: each fixed‚Äë`Œ∏` slice and Type A are charged into the separated `2^{(Œ≤/H)Q_H}` term (`rem:aper`); my claim concerns `Œõ^aper`. Contained incidences: noncontainment automatic (Cycle95). Same‚Äëslope `P(Œ±)=P'(Œ±)`: only shrinks `|eval_Œ±(L_w)|` below `|List|`, so it can only help the upper bound (adversary takes `Œ±‚àâD` off the `‚â§k` roots of each pairwise difference). Affine color: scaling `U` rescales `Œ∏` bijectively (count‚Äëinvariant); additive translation does not preserve `H`, so is unavailable ‚Äî and crucially none of these removes the external‚Äëroot continuum, which is the whole obstruction.
6. **PLAN‚ÜíPROOF / COUNTERPACKET.** PROOF: establish Lemma S1‚ÄëB1 via a Stepanov/Weil incidence bound for the degree‚Äë`(œÉ+1)` curve `Œì` against the prefix image, with periodic `Œ∏` charged; combine with the `b=0` local limit and induct on `b`. COUNTERPACKET: a monic degree‚Äë`(s+1)` `U` over a generated prime field, above reserve, whose curve `Œì` sits on a coset translate yielding superpolynomially many aperiodic active `Œ∏` ‚Äî detected by the ¬ß4.2 toy sweep showing superpolynomial growth of `|Œò_U^{aper}|`.
